verdict.answer_first: A states the plan immediately: “get TypeScript tooling in place first… then convert file-by-file behind a strictness ratchet”; B opens with the warning “Migrating 30k lines all at once is the trap to avoid” before the plan.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to a concrete default flow with commands/config and rules: “Keep your existing bundler… Use tsc --noEmit purely as the type checker” and “No behavior changes in a conversion PR.”
verdict.fabrication: B invents repo context: “this one (`fable-profile`) looks like an eval project,” which the question never gave.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on now because it includes install commands, a starter tsconfig, CI step, per-file loop, strictness order, and dependency-graph command.
